issues.title: Side Yard Setback Variance - 3524 Gregory St
issues.summary: A property owner at 3524 Gregory St in Alder District #13 is requesting a side yard setback variance to add an addition to a single-family home. This is a routine zoning variance petition that will be heard by the Zoning Board of Appeals.

public_comment.instructions: Contact Zoning Board of Appeals or city planning department for hearing details

issues.why_it_matters_to_you: This is a single-property variance with minimal citywide impact. Unless you live in District #13 or have specific concerns about neighborhood character, this is routine administrative business.

issues.title: Appeal of Landmarks Commission Approval - 113 Ely Place Land Division
issues.summary: An appeal has been filed against the Landmarks Commission's approval of a land division at 113 Ely Place in the University Heights Historic District. The Common Council will review whether the land division complies with historic preservation standards. This is coming to a council vote on July 21, 2026.

public_comment.instructions: Public comment accepted at Common Council meeting on July 21, 2026. Check Madison.legistar.com for meeting details.

issues.why_it_matters_to_you: This touches on housing development and community preservation—both topics where you value stability. However, since you're persuadable on housing if framed right, this represents the kind of nuanced decision (balancing growth vs. preservation) that aligns with your pragmatic approach.

issues.title: Downtown Area Plan Review
issues.summary: The Madison Arts Commission is reviewing the Downtown Area Plan, currently in committee. This plan likely addresses arts, culture, and public space in Madison's downtown core.

public_comment.instructions: Contact Madison Arts Commission for meeting schedule and public comment opportunities

issues.why_it_matters_to_you: Downtown revitalization affects community character and quality of life—areas you care about. However, this is early-stage and in a primarily advisory body, so your direct influence is limited at this point.

issues.title: Public Works Transportation Projects Review - Parman Terrace (Cul-de-Sac to Odana Rd)
issues.summary: The Transportation Commission is reviewing a public works transportation project on Parman Terrace, involving street work from the cul-de-sac to Odana Road. This is in committee and appears to address infrastructure planning or street improvements.

public_comment.instructions: Contact Transportation Commission for meeting schedule

issues.why_it_matters_to_you: This is a neighborhood-level infrastructure project. Unless you live on or near Parman Terrace, this has limited salience to your broader civic interests.

issues.title: No Right on Red Review and Discussion
issues.summary: The Transportation Commission is discussing a potential policy on 'No Right on Red' traffic regulations. This would restrict right turns at red lights in certain locations to improve pedestrian and bicycle safety. The item has been in committee since April 24, 2026.

public_comment.instructions: Contact Transportation Commission for meeting schedule and public comment opportunities

issues.why_it_matters_to_you: This touches on both safety and collective good (protecting pedestrians)—values you hold. However, it's still in early discussion with no imminent vote, so it's worth monitoring but doesn't require immediate action.

issues.title: Common Council Meeting Formats Through September 2027
issues.summary: The Council Office has issued a series of reports scheduling and confirming the meeting formats (hybrid virtual and in-person at City-County Building Room 201) for Madison Common Council meetings through September 21, 2027. These are procedural scheduling matters.

public_comment.instructions: These are procedural/scheduling items; public comment not typically applicable

issues.why_it_matters_to_you: The hybrid format (virtual + in-person) aligns with your low engagement appetite—you can participate without attending in person if issues matter to you. This is useful logistical information but not a substantive policy issue.
